{"idea": {"Name": "implicit_latent_optimization_tts", "Title": "Implicit Latent Optimization: Zero-Perturbation Test-Time Scaling under Strict Latency Budgets", "Short Hypothesis": "Test-time scaling can be achieved without explicit activation perturbation or multi-sample generation by formulating inference as a constrained implicit optimization over activation space. A single, closed-form latent update—derived from minimizing token-level entropy using only current forward-pass intermediates—implicitly steers generation toward optimal trajectories, yielding accuracy gains comparable to Best-of-N while enforcing strict wall-clock latency bounds and zero perturbation overhead. Autoregressive reasoning is the ideal setting because compounding trajectory deviations are fundamentally optimization failures; simpler interventions (prompting, sampling, or explicit steering) either lack latency guarantees or introduce distribution shift, making a closed-form, in
